title: Module 7 knowledge check
markdown: Map scenarios to NIST trustworthiness characteristics and responsible practice.

questions.prompt: Which source defines the trustworthiness characteristics used in this module?
"Fair — with harmful bias managed" is best matched to which concern?
choices: Systematic unfair outcomes tied to data or design

questions.prompt: Human oversight in a customer-email drafting scenario most nearly means:
choices: A person reviews or approves messages before they send when stakes warrant it
Never reading what the model wrote

questions.prompt: Privacy-enhanced AI practice includes minimizing unnecessary personal data in prompts and tools.
If a chatbot invents a fake policy quote in a support draft, which characteristic is most directly at risk?
A practical way to keep learning after this course is to:
choices: Return to primary sources like the Stanford HAI AI Index and NIST AI RMF for updates
Trust every social headline without checking dates
Clarifying who is responsible and what the system's limits are
Hiding all failure modes from users forever
Claiming the model is always right
